title: Finding-ID-shaped attribution tags in shipped source collide with the active 017 review's own finding-ID namespace
evidence: dispatch-model.cjs:145 '(traceability-3-5)', :381 '(maintainability-8-4)'; run-benchmark.cjs:409,455,466,503 carry traceability-4-2/7-5/7-2 tags. The cited 014 review emitted every finding as "id":"n" (no dimension-iter-N IDs); these IDs were synthesized in the same dimension-iter-N form the live 017 review emits, causing a grep-collision.
fix: Rewrite the 5 tags to a namespace-safe form (e.g. '014 review F-n, iter N') that does not reuse the bare dimension-iter-N shape an active deep-review emits; optionally lint-forbid unqualified dimension-iter-N tags in source.

reason: documented smoke-test seam; public API is score(criteria-as-data) consumed by run-benchmark via require(); not a defect
cross-checked references/agent-improvement/score_dimensions.md:163 — both agree on p126; internally consistent

direction: scorer references point at n.cjs instead of score-model-variant.cjs
reason: cat -v verification confirmed run-benchmark.cjs:431 and YAML:91 correctly reference score-model-variant.cjs; earlier n.cjs was a ripgrep display artifact

detail: 015 fixes re-confirmed sound this round: dispatcher diagnostics (dispatch-model.cjs:381-395), repo-relative resume hint (148-155), Atomics.wait sleepSync (122-130), packet-local candidate-keyed score cache (score-candidate.cjs:171-181,554-555), report-history snapshot (run-benchmark.cjs:508-511)
